description: Perform a comprehensive ROC diagnostic accuracy analysis on the Wisconsin Breast Cancer dataset (`mlbench::BreastCancer`) to identify the best single cytological biomarker and compare it to a combined logistic regression model. Create an R script at `/home/ga/RProjects/breast_roc_analysis.R` using the `pROC` and `mlbench` packages. Process the dataset by performing complete-case analysis for `Bare.nuclei` and converting the nine cytological features from factors to numeric values (1-10). Generate `/home/ga/RProjects/output/breast_roc_individual.csv` containing ROC metrics (feature, auc, auc_lower95, auc_upper95, optimal_cutpoint, sensitivity, specificity) for each feature. Compare the best single feature against the other eight using DeLong's test and save the results (feature1, feature2, auc1, auc2, z_statistic, p_value) to `/home/ga/RProjects/output/breast_auc_comparison.csv`. Fit a logistic regression model using all nine features and save its ROC performance (model, auc, auc_lower95, auc_upper95, sensitivity, specificity, accuracy, n_observations) to `/home/ga/RProjects/output/breast_combined_model.csv`. Create a multi-panel visualization (minimum 1200x900px) at `/home/ga/RProjects/output/breast_roc_analysis.png` featuring ROC curves for all individual features (Panel A), the ROC curve for the combined model (Panel B), and a bar chart comparing AUCs of all features and the combined model (Panel C).
